methods.description: Process for isolating the putative promoter sequence of the Zm908 gene from maize variety Zong31.

steps.description: The putative promoter sequence of Zm908, designated here as pZm908, was cloned from Zong31.
steps.searchable_summary: Clone Zm908 putative promoter sequence from maize genomic DNA.
outputs: Cloned pZm908 sequence (likely in a vector)

steps.description: It was 2,126 bp in length, comprising 2,068 nucleotides immediately upstream from the putative transcription start site of the Zm908 gene and 58 nucleotides of the 5 untranslated region of the Zm908 gene.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine the length and composition of the cloned pZm908 sequence.

methods.description: Analysis of the cloned pZm908 sequence using bioinformatics tools to identify potential cis-regulatory elements.

steps.description: Bioinformatics analysis of pZm908 identified several pollen-specific cis-regulatory elements, including the AGAAA motif, the GTGA motif and the Q-element (Table 1).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform bioinformatics analysis on pZm908 sequence to identify cis-regulatory elements.
outputs: List of identified potential cis-regulatory elements and their locations

steps.description: AGAAA is a pollen-specific transcription activation cis-regulatory element... The GTGA motif, conserved... participates in directing pollen-specific expression... The Q-element can enhance the expression...
steps.searchable_summary: Identify and characterize known pollen-specific motifs (AGAAA, GTGA, Q-element) within the pZm908 sequence.
inputs: List of identified potential cis-regulatory elements
outputs: Confirmation and location of specific pollen-specific motifs (AGAAA, GTGA, Q-element) in pZm908
Association of motifs with known functions

methods.method_name: pZm908 Truncation Series and Construct Construction
methods.description: Creation of a series of truncated pZm908 promoter sequences and their ligation into a GUS reporter vector to identify functional regions.

steps.description: To identify functional regions in pZm908, a promoter deletion series (p908, p1670, p1126, p789, p586, p244, p184, and p126) was prepared as outlined in Figure 1A...
steps.searchable_summary: Generate a series of truncated pZm908 DNA fragments.
outputs: DNA fragments representing the pZm908 truncation series

steps.description: ...and used to generate GUS reporter gene constructs.
steps.searchable_summary: Ligate each truncated pZm908 fragment into a GUS reporter vector.
outputs: GUS reporter constructs containing the pZm908 truncation series

methods.description: Transformation of tobacco plants with the GUS reporter constructs to generate stable transgenic lines for expression analysis.

steps.description: These constructs were analyzed using a tobacco system due to the sufficient quantity of pollen grains and the high transformation efficiency of this system, which enabled many promoter variants to be evaluated in detail as stably transformed lines.
steps.searchable_summary: Transform tobacco plants with the GUS reporter constructs.

steps.description: ...enabled many promoter variants to be evaluated in detail as stably transformed lines.
steps.searchable_summary: Generate stable transgenic tobacco lines expressing the GUS reporter constructs.

methods.description: Analysis of GUS reporter gene expression patterns in different tissues of transgenic tobacco plants using histochemical staining.

steps.description: We performed GUS staining in the leaves and roots of seedlings, mature pollen grains, indehiscent anthers and immature seeds collected from the transgenic plants.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect specific tissue samples from transgenic tobacco plants.
outputs: Collected tissue samples (leaves, roots, pollen, anthers, seeds)

steps.description: We performed GUS staining... following staining with X-gluc...
steps.searchable_summary: Perform GUS staining on collected tissue samples using X-gluc.

steps.description: The results showed that no apparent GUS activity was observed following staining with X-gluc in seedling roots, immature seeds or indehiscent anthers for any construct (Figure 1). p1670 and p1126 showed strong GUS staining in seedling leaves. GUS staining was detected in mature pollen with all constructs except p126. GUS staining was found in both mature pollen grains and leaves of seedlings for p1670 and p1126... The full-length pZm908, p908, showed high GUS staining intensity in mature pollen grains, whereas no apparent GUS staining was detected in the other four tissues...
steps.searchable_summary: Observe and record the presence and intensity of GUS activity (blue staining) in each tissue sample for each construct.
outputs: Qualitative data on GUS expression presence/intensity per tissue per construct

steps.description: ...indicating that p908 was pollen-specific. The p126 deletion constructs... showed no GUS staining in mature pollen grains, indicating that the -126 to -68 region was crucial for pollen expression.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze GUS expression data to determine promoter activity patterns, tissue specificity, and identify functional regulatory regions.
inputs: Qualitative GUS expression data for all constructs and tissues
outputs: Identification of promoter regions driving specific expression patterns (e.g., pollen-specific)
Identification of crucial regulatory regions (e.g., -126 to -68 region)
